title: myrrhis pecten-veneris
intro: Myrrhis pecten-veneris, also known as Carrot Lovage or Lovage, is a perennial herb native to Europe, belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its expectorant, diuretic, and anti-inflammatory effects. It has been traditionally used to alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis, as well as to support digestive health and reduce swelling. Lovage can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often consumed fresh in salads or used as a cooking herb to add flavor and potential health benefits.
